title: Nachträge zu den Legenden
summary: The General Corpus XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00063. That item selectively encodes five independently published Carl Horstmann installments from Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 62 (1879), 68 (1882), 74 (1885), 79 (1887), and 80 (1888), not one medieval work and not the complete serialized Nachträge run. The five blocks print twenty-seven leaf-level manuscript streams from more than twenty codices, sometimes through transcripts supplied by Eugen Kölbing, Frederick Furnivall, or an unidentified friend. Complete whole-object caches retain five journal volumes and the publicly obtainable complete physical-source surrogates for Digby 86, the Vernon Manuscript, Sion L40.2/E25, Ashmole 61, Bodley 779, and Huntington HM 114; exact target folios and digital ranges are mapped inside those full objects. Bodley 596, Douce 322, and Douce 372 expose only provider selections, which are completely cached but correctly remain partial manuscript coverage, with target overlap recorded only for Bodley 596 f. 1r. Remaining sources have dated no-public-complete-copy findings and whole-object reproduction routes.
review_notes: The first XML DIV1 joins the Archiv 62 article to only the final, pp. 207–224 installment in Archiv 68. Volume 68 also contains an unencoded earlier continuation beginning p. 52; CME00063 is therefore a selection, not the complete serial.

Archiv 79 pp. 424–432 prints the Vernon and Harley Long Charter witnesses in parallel columns. The XML serializes them consecutively and repeats the physical page references; p. 424 is also shared with the preceding incest tale.

Archiv 80 begins on printed p. 114 and physically interleaves three calendar witnesses: Douce on pp. 114, 116, and odd pp. 119–135; Rawlinson on pp. 115, 117, and repeated odd pp. 119–135; Harley on even pp. 118–134. Repeated PB values preserve that presentation rather than duplication error.

Historical identities are retained but resolved: Cheltenham/Phillipps 8252 is Huntington HM 114; the Marquis of Bath/Bath MS 25 is Longleat MS 257; Horstmann's Douce 872 is a bibliographic error for Douce 372; Sion L40.2/E25 is now held by Lambeth Palace Library.

No assigned direct witness is classified as lost. Negative access findings are dated and say no public complete copy found, because institutional holdings, legacy viewers, and mediated reproductions can change.

Bayerische Staatsbibliothek metadata describes these journal volumes as Berlin: E. Schmidt, while the digitized title pages visibly identify Braunschweig: George Westermann. Edition bibliographic facts follow the physical title pages; the provider-catalog discrepancy remains noted.

Whole-object requests should specify boards or covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf, edges or detached matter when offered, and any existing microfilm. Target work folios must be mapped separately rather than used to crop the request.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00063
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by the repository publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00063
entities.description: Public digital text selecting five Horstmann serial blocks, with German editorial matter, Middle English, Anglo-Norman, and Latin texts, notes, page and folio milestones, and italic expansion or supply typography.

entities.label: Horstmann, Nachträge zu den Legenden, Archiv 62 (1879)
entities.description: The first immediate source block encoded in CME00063: seven separately transmitted witness texts plus Add MS 22283 variant apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Nachträge zu den Legenden,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 62 (1879), 397–431 (Braunschweig: George Westermann, 1879).

bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen

entities.label: Horstmann, Nachträge zu den Legenden (Schluß), Archiv 68 (1882)
entities.description: The final pp. 207–224 installment selected by CME00063, containing the Sion Gospel of Nicodemus; the volume's earlier p. 52 continuation is outside the encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Nachträge zu den Legenden (Schluß),' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 68 (1882), 207–224 (Braunschweig: George Westermann, 1882).

entities.description: The third selected block: Childhood of Jesus and Susanna through Kölbing transcripts, and two prose Adam versions, the first through Furnivall's transcript.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Nachträge zu den Legenden,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 74 (1885), 327–365 (Braunschweig: George Westermann, 1885).

entities.label: Horstmann, Nachträge zu den Legenden, Archiv 79 (1887)
entities.description: The fourth selected block, printing twelve witness streams including two parallel Charter texts and two Latin Adam/cross texts.

entities.label: Complete digital facsimiles of Archiv 62 (1879)
entities.description: Complete Internet Archive PDF and complete BSB 506-canvas digital volume; Horstmann's article is mapped inside each whole journal object.

entities.label: Complete digital facsimiles of Archiv 68 (1882)
entities.description: Complete Internet Archive PDF and complete BSB 514-canvas digital volume; the encoded final installment and omitted earlier continuation remain visible in the whole object.

entities.label: Complete digital facsimiles of Archiv 74 (1885)
entities.description: Complete Internet Archive PDF and complete BSB 504-canvas digital volume.

entities.label: Complete digital facsimiles of Archiv 79 (1887)
entities.description: Complete Internet Archive PDF and complete BSB 504-canvas digital volume, retaining the article's physical parallel-column layout.

entities.label: Complete digital facsimiles of Archiv 80 (1888)
entities.description: Complete Internet Archive PDF and complete BSB 512-canvas digital volume, retaining the three physically interleaved calendar streams.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.2.6
entities.description: Direct source for the Scottish Legendary Saint Alexius at ff. 163r–169r; the last leaf is shared with the following Saint Julian. Horstmann's headnote f. 164b is erroneous, while internal milestones begin at f. 163v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Digby 86
entities.description: Direct source for Les ounsse peines de enfer / The Eleven Pains of Hell at ff. 132r–134v.

notes: Produced in Worcestershire; contains English, Latin, and Old French.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II
entities.description: Direct base for the imperfect Pistill of Susan at current ff. 3r–5r; Horstmann uses the old fol. 1 and notes that the opening 104 lines are lost. The current 210-folio binding contains two historical units.

holding.notes: Component record supports the target; the parent record supports the complete 210-folio physical object.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.1.1
entities.description: Direct source for Horstmann's complete Saint Erasmus text. Best current scholarship gives ff. 295r–296v; the 1856 institutional catalog uses manuscript pp. 537–540, while an 1869 handlist maps ff. 258r–259v.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.4.9
entities.description: Direct source for the first complete Robert of Sicily witness version, ff. 87v–93v.

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 174/95
entities.description: Direct source for the second complete Robert of Sicily witness version, manuscript pp. 456–468.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: Direct source for the third complete Robert of Sicily witness version at modern ff. 254r–257v. Horstmann's fol. 93ff. remains an unresolved legacy or error locator.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, Sion L40.2/E25
entities.description: Direct source for the strophic Gospel of Nicodemus at ff. 13r–38v; Horstmann gives ff. 13–39. Folio 31 survives only as a small fragment.

physical_description.support: Paper, with a reused parchment binding leaf at f. 134
physical_description.extent: ii + 133 + ii folios, plus reused binding leaf f. 134

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 31042
entities.description: Direct manuscript source for the Childhood of Jesus, ff. 163v–168v, mediated by Eugen Kölbing's transcript. The terminal verso is inferred from the next item beginning f. 169.

entities.label: Huntington Library MS HM 114
entities.description: Direct source for the later Susanna text, ff. 184v–190v, mediated by Eugen Kölbing's transcript. The same codex contains Piers Plowman, Mandeville, Troilus, and other works.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodl. 596
entities.description: Direct source for two CME00063 prose texts: the first Life of Adam at catalog ff. 1r–11v, with an XML milestone continuing onto f. 12, and the Lamentation of Our Lady at ff. 17r–21r.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4775
entities.description: Direct source for the second Life of Adam and Eve version, an addition to the Gilte Legende, at scholarly ff. 258v–264r. The current catalog's Gilte Legende extent ends f. 262v, so the endpoint requires reconciliation.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodl. 779
entities.description: Direct source for Seint Margarete þe holy virgine at ff. 204v–208r, within a South English Legendary extending through f. 307r.

date_statements.label: Current catalog date
date_statements.value: Second quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: ii + 312 folios, with separate binding fragments
notes: The complete digital project includes detached former-binding leather and parchment strengtheners.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Rawl. poet. 118
entities.description: Direct source for the Punishment of Adultery at ff. 113v–116r and Tale of an Incestuous Daughter at ff. 116r–118v. The abbreviated current catalog's ff. 4v–6v appears to have dropped the leading 11 and is preserved as a conflict.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Eng. poet. a.1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: Direct source for the Long Charter at ff. 317v–318r and Three Messengers of Death at ff. 297v–298r; also the previously edited Susanna base at f. 317r–v against which the volume-62 Cotton supplement and Add MS 22283 variants are contextualized.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2382
entities.description: Direct source for the second Long Charter / Testamentum Christi version, ff. 112r–118r; the following Childe of Bristowe begins f. 118v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: Direct source for the Founding of the Feasts of All Saints and All Souls at ff. 73r–78v and the unique Romance of Christ's Resurrection at ff. 138v–144v.

entities.label: Longleat House MS 257
alternate_labels: Manuscript of the Marquis of Bath
entities.description: Direct source for De matre et VII pueris at ff. 209r–212r, the final episode of the Middle English Metrical Paraphrase of the Old Testament, mediated by an unidentified friend's transcript used by Horstmann.

physical_description.extent: 184 surviving leaves in 212 collational positions, 28 lost, plus two modern flyleaves

entities.label: Queen's College Oxford MS 213
entities.description: Direct source for Horstmann's Latin Vita prothoplasti Adae at ff. 1r–7v. The codex was copied by Nicholas Warde in 1449; the following De ligno sanctae crucis item begins f. 8 and supplies the subsequent printed section with supported, not fully reconciled, scope.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4011
entities.description: Direct source for the March–December calendar version at ff. 138r–142v; its January–February opening leaf is missing. The current catalog attributes A Kalendare to John Lydgate.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Douce 322
entities.description: Direct source for the complete January–December calendar version at ff. 2r–7v, which Horstmann judges the best of the three texts.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Rawl. B. 408
entities.description: Direct source for the Oxford-dialect calendar version at preliminary ff. 7r–12v, before the Godstow cartulary proper.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript)
entities.description: Variant/collation witness for the first Susanna section at current ff. 125v–126r, not its copy text. Horstmann's legacy left-side foliation calls the location fol. 326; the codex also contains collateral Robert and Childhood texts.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 876
entities.description: Cited incomplete parallel witness to the Gilte Legende Life of Adam, not the CME copy text. Modern sources disagree between f. 316 and f. 321r.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Douce 372
entities.description: Cited incomplete parallel witness to the Gilte Legende Life of Adam at ff. 158r–161v; Horstmann's Douce 872 is a bibliographic error.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Rawl. C. 499
entities.description: Only a passage-scoped cited parallel for a note on Adam's body/name at f. 153; it is not the source of a complete CME00063 text.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing's transcript of Add MS 31042
entities.description: The transcript Horstmann explicitly thanks Kölbing for and uses as the immediate textual intermediary for Childhood of Jesus.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing's transcript of former Cheltenham 8252
entities.description: The transcript Horstmann explicitly thanks Kölbing for and uses as the immediate textual intermediary for the Huntington HM 114 Susanna.

entities.label: Frederick Furnivall's transcript of the Bodley 596 Life of Adam
entities.description: The transcript Horstmann explicitly credits as his intermediary for the first Life of Adam version.

entities.label: Unidentified friend's transcript of Longleat MS 257
entities.description: The immediate copy used by Horstmann for De matre et VII pueris; Horstmann says only that it was a friend's transcript.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Digby 86
entities.description: The complete 430-canvas object from upper cover through lower cover, including endleaves, blanks, all 207 folios, the target text, and adjacent contents.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 430 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: A complete 710-canvas color surrogate including covers, flyleaves, surviving leaves, and final spine canvas. Three discontiguous CME00063 portions are mapped inside it.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 710 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Lambeth LUNA complete digital book for Sion L40.2/E25
entities.description: The official 279-image compound-book sequence includes upper/lower covers, inside covers, flyleaves, every surviving folio including blanks and fragments, adjacent works, and the reused binding leaf. The associated IIIF manifest has no canvases, so the complete MediaManager image inventory is bundled.
entities.reproduction_format: LUNA MediaManager JPEG images / complete 279-image inventory

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete exposed image sequence for MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: All 358 provider canvases from upper board through former lower board; both discontiguous CME00063 target works are mapped inside it.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 358 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete project facsimile of MS Bodley 779
entities.description: The complete 672-canvas published project inventory includes boards, endleaves, every manuscript leaf, parchment strengtheners, and detached original-binding leather.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 672 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Huntington complete digital facsimile of MS HM 114
entities.description: The complete 679-canvas provider sequence includes current and former covers, spine, flyleaves, removed binding, cord stations, every exposed manuscript leaf, the target Susanna, and adjacent works.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 679 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Digital Bodleian 13-image selection for MS Bodley 596
entities.description: The complete provider selection but only partial manuscript coverage. Folio 1r overlaps the Life of Adam opening; the rest of that text and the entire Lamentation are absent.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian four-image selection for MS Douce 322
entities.description: The complete four-canvas provider selection but partial manuscript coverage. Its f. 15r, f. 19v, and two f. 78r images have zero overlap with the calendar at ff. 2r–7v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian one-image selection for MS Douce 372
entities.description: The complete one-canvas provider selection but partial manuscript coverage. Folio 163r has zero overlap with the cited Life of Adam fragment at ff. 158r–161v.

scope.notes: The earlier continuation beginning on printed p. 52 is outside the encoding.

access.access_method: Request the entire current physical codex from CUL Imaging Services, then map ff. 163r–169r separately.
notes: No public complete CUDL/IIIF object was found on the review date.
Ask for boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all 395 leaves, edges/detached matter when available, and separate reuse terms.

access.access_method: Request the entire current physical codex and ask CUL to reconcile the three Erasmus locators.
notes: No public complete CUDL/IIIF object was found.
Target mapping requires direct confirmation of ff. 295r–296v versus old pp. 537–540 and ff. 258r–259v.

access.access_method: Request the entire current physical codex; map Robert of Sicily at ff. 87v–93v separately.
notes: No public complete CUDL/IIIF object was found.

access.access_method: Request the entire current physical codex and ask CUL to reconcile modern ff. 254r–257v with Horstmann's fol. 93ff.

editorial_practices.description: CME00063 combines five Horstmann installments but omits at least the earlier continuation beginning on Archiv 68 p. 52; its four DIV1 elements are not equivalent to the five immediate edition blocks.
Horstmann assembles separately transmitted saints' lives, exempla, Charters, feast poems, an Old Testament episode, a Marian lament, and Latin Adam/cross texts under one recurring article title.
editorial_practices.scope: Archiv 79 installment; representative of the serial's heterogeneous compilation practice

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscripts not all inspected directly by Horstmann
editorial_practices.representation: Copy texts supplied through Kölbing and Furnivall transcripts
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann explicitly credits Kölbing's Add 31042 and Cheltenham 8252 transcripts and Furnivall's Bodley 596 transcript. The manuscript remains the direct textual witness, but the transcript is an intermediate material layer.
editorial_practices.scope: Childhood of Jesus, Susanna, and first Life of Adam

editorial_practices.source_feature: Incomplete Cotton Susanna, previous Vernon edition, and Add MS 22283 readings
editorial_practices.representation: Cotton copy text followed by separately marked Add MS 22283 variant apparatus
editorial_practices.description: The article distinguishes its incomplete Cotton base from Add MS 22283 variants and its earlier Vernon-edition context rather than silently constructing one witness.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Vernon and Harley Long Charter versions
editorial_practices.description: The two complete witness versions are printed side by side; the XML serializes them and repeats PB values, so neither stream should be treated as apparatus to the other.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three calendar manuscripts, Harley lacking January–February
editorial_practices.representation: Physical interleaving by page and parallel columns; XML serializes three witness streams
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann says Douce is generally best and Harley a reworking, but also states that the original cannot be reconstructed from these three witnesses alone. The manifest preserves all three versions independently.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Horstmann's italic expansion, supply, and editorial typography
editorial_practices.representation: 9,788 HI elements with REND='italic'; no reconstruction of manuscript abbreviation signs
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves printed italics at scale, but italics are editorial data and cannot by themselves reveal the original manuscript glyphs or abbreviation marks.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00063.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00063 and preserves the five selected journal blocks, source headings, texts, witnesses, page and folio milestones, intermediary-transcript credits, notes, italics, and structural review history.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural and identifier audit of CME00063.xml.
evidence.locator: ETS/EEBO/TEXT element counts, hierarchy, PB sequence, and source hash
evidence.summary: Counts 4 DIV1, 26 DIV2, 30 DIV3, 71 HEAD, 22 HEADNOTE, 798 LG, 9,849 L, 62 P, 193 PB, 2,121 MILESTONE, 556 NOTE, 9,788 italic HI, 12 TABLE, 365 ROW, and 1,825 CELL elements; confirms the five serial blocks and repeated physical pages in the Charter and calendar encodings.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Nachträge zu den legenden, CME00063.
evidence.summary: Provides the public item, title, Carl Horstmann attribution, Braunschweig/Westermann 1879–1888 publication summary, public-domain statement, and DPLA No Copyright–United States assertion.

evidence.citation: Complete Internet Archive scans of Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen, volumes 62, 68, 74, 79, and 80.
evidence.summary: Confirms the five independent article identities, exact pages 397–431, 207–224, 327–365, 411–470, and 114–135, title-page publication facts, source headings, witness credits, transcript acknowledgments, and the omitted earlier volume-68 continuation.
notes: Complete PDFs, scandata, and item metadata are checksum-pinned in the corresponding access records.

evidence.citation: Visual audit of all five complete Archiv volume scans and BSB equivalents.
evidence.locator: Volume title pages; article first/last pages; Archiv 68 TOC and p. 52; Archiv 79 pp. 424–432; Archiv 80 pp. 114–135
evidence.summary: Confirms article boundaries, Braunschweig/Westermann imprints, volume-68 selective serial boundary, the two-column Long Charter presentation, p. 424 shared boundary, and three physically interleaved calendar streams beginning on p. 114.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata and scandata for the five complete journal scans.
evidence.summary: Supplies exact file inventories, page mappings, and access-specific license evidence: volumes 62 and 79 carry Public Domain Mark 1.0; volumes 68, 74, and 80 expose no item-level license.

evidence.citation: BSB IIIF Presentation manifests bsb11392481, bsb11874325, bsb11874331, bsb11874336, and bsb11874337.
evidence.summary: Confirms complete 506-, 514-, 504-, 504-, and 512-canvas digital volumes, article canvas ranges, provider catalog data, and exact NoC-NC 1.0 manifest assertions.
notes: The five manifest files are cached and checksum-pinned in their access records.

evidence.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vols. 1 and 3 (1856–1858), entries for Dd.1.1 and Gg.2.6.
evidence.locator: Gg.2.6 description and item boundaries; Dd.1.1 item 8 at manuscript pp. 537–540
evidence.summary: Supports current-form CUL shelfmarks, Gg.2.6's 395 paper leaves and Alexius/Saint Julian boundary, and the Dd.1.1 Saint Erasmus pagination; current classmark guidance supports dotted Arabic forms.
notes: Volume 1 is separately cached at source-cache/CME00063/cambridge-manuscripts-volume-1-1856.pdf, SHA-256 a146b2b31e1410174ef2f2753f5150c906c21486a5c1193faf4465fcd694e84c.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Gonville and Caius College, vol. 1 (1907), p. 198, MS 174/95.
evidence.locator: Printed p. 198 / PDF p. 238; Robert of Cesyll at manuscript p. 456
evidence.summary: Confirms the combined shelfmark 174/95, Robert of Sicily manuscript pages 456–468, incipit, explicit, and neighboring contents.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Robert of Cisyle, with Cambridge and METS witness records.
evidence.summary: Supports the three independent Robert witness identities and modern loci, the Ff.2.38 former More 690 shelfmark, and romance/date/region context without resolving Horstmann's Ff fol. 93ff. conflict.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue records for CME00063 witnesses.
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmarks and physical records for Cotton Caligula A II, Additional 22283 and 31042, Harley 4775, Egerton 876, Harley 2382, and Harley 4011; identifies current target components where cataloged and records absent or unavailable digitised content.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services, order form, Imaging Services terms, and rate card.
evidence.summary: Supports whole-object request routes, quote-dependent difficult-item photography, fees, required shelfmark/format details, and the separation of supplied-image access from written reproduction permission.

evidence.citation: Mary Frances Wellesley, thesis evidence for the Long Charter in Harley MS 2382.
evidence.locator: Testamentum Christi begins f. 112r; following Childe of Bristowe begins f. 118v
evidence.summary: Bounds the direct Harley Long Charter source to ff. 112r–118r.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Catalogue of Western Medieval Manuscripts records for CME00063 witnesses.
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmarks, holdings, dates, physical descriptions, content loci, and current digital-object links for Digby 86, Vernon, Ashmole 61, Bodley 596, Bodley 779, Rawlinson poetry 118, Douce 322, Douce 372, Rawlinson C. 499, and Rawlinson B. 408.
notes: Current TEI source files for seven records are cached and checksum-pinned in their catalog access records.

evidence.citation: Exact Digital Bodleian IIIF manifests and terms for CME00063 facsimile objects.
evidence.summary: Each exact object attributes its photographs to Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0. Manifest canvas inventories distinguish complete Digby/Vernon/Ashmole/Bodley 779 objects from selected Bodley 596/Douce 322/Douce 372 objects.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Imaging Services, mediated copying, and copyright/reproducing-images guidance.
evidence.summary: Supports mediated whole-object reproduction requests and shows that newly supplied images and commercial/non-academic reuse require terms distinct from public Digital Bodleian objects.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete MS Digby 86 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms cover-to-cover exposed-object coverage, exact target start/end, adjacent-work boundary at f. 135r, color access derivatives, and complete inventory integrity.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete Vernon Manuscript IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: 710 canvases; Three Messengers 481–482; Susanna 520–521; Long Charter 521–522
evidence.summary: Confirms complete exposed-object coverage and exact discontiguous work mappings, including Susanna/Charter overlap on f. 317v.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete MS Ashmole 61 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms complete exposed-object coverage and exact discontiguous target ranges at ff. 73r–78v and 138v–144v.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries catalog and Digital Bodleian object for MS Bodl. 779.
evidence.summary: Confirms second-quarter fifteenth-century date, paper support, ii + 312 folios, South English Legendary ff. 1r–307r, former-binding fragments, and the new 672-canvas complete project created in February 2026.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete MS Bodley 779 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms all manifest canvases were retained, including boards, endleaves, every leaf, strengtheners, and detached leather; visually confirms the target range and following Saint Oswin boundary.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian 13-canvas selected-image manifest and complete local selection bundle for MS Bodl. 596.
evidence.locator: All 13 selection canvases; f. 1r overlap only
evidence.summary: Confirms complete capture of the provider selection but partial manuscript coverage: only f. 1r overlaps the Life of Adam and no canvas overlaps the Lamentation.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian selected-image manifests for MSS Douce 322 and Douce 372.
evidence.locator: Douce 322 all four canvases; Douce 372 sole canvas
evidence.summary: Confirms complete capture of both provider selections but partial manuscript coverage and zero target overlap: Douce 322 selection excludes calendar ff. 2r–7v; Douce 372 f. 163r excludes Adam ff. 158r–161v.
notes: The separately cached Douce 372 selection is source-cache/CME00063/bodleian-ms-douce-372-selected-complete-iiif-1800px.zip, SHA-256 8e34eeba8b6763df7a0285faf2fa3b2e04055bfb4dcf524913d644839ab8bc81.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Archives Catalogue and LUNA compound-book object for Sion L40.2/E25.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current Lambeth holding, Sion shelfmark, fourteenth–fifteenth-century paper codex, extent, Gospel ff. 13r–38v, fragmentary f. 31, 279-page public book object, and terse image-rights value LPL.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete Sion L40.2/E25 LUNA bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms cover-to-cover current-object inventory including blanks, fragments, adjacent works, and reused binding leaf; verifies the target start and end despite the empty-canvas metadata manifest.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library Digital Photography and Permission to Reproduce forms.
evidence.summary: Documents contacts, formats, fees, private-research undertaking, ordinary 40-scan limit, and separate publication permission.
notes: Permission form separately cached, SHA-256 9d659518492dcbc53f76304e4953242357f39b43102a3a02face8b061cfc85e4.

evidence.citation: Huntington Library catalog and digital object for mssHM 114.
evidence.summary: Resolves Cheltenham/Phillipps 8252 to current HM 114, dates the English manuscript to the mid-fifteenth century, identifies the mixed contents, exposes a 679-canvas complete physical-object sequence, and gives license:null with a rights-policy link.

evidence.citation: Inventory and representative-image audit of the complete Huntington HM 114 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Confirms retention of all canvases including current/former covers, spine, flyleaves, removed binding, cord stations, all leaves, target boundaries, adjacent works, and color 1600-pixel access derivatives.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that Huntington does not require permission to publish public-domain material, asks for item citation, leaves copyright analysis and third-party permissions to the researcher, and provides no object-specific open license for HM 114 photography.

evidence.citation: Eugen Kölbing, description of the Marquis of Bath manuscript, Englische Studien 10 (1887), 203–206.
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 203–206; De matre et VII pueris at f. 209r
evidence.summary: Identifies current Longleat MS 257/former Bath MS 25, itemizes the Middle English Metrical Paraphrase, bounds De matre et VII pueris at ff. 209r–212r, and shows the codex does not contain the speculatively queried Susanna.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Middle English Metrical Paraphrase of the Old Testament.
evidence.summary: Identifies the target as the final episode of the larger paraphrase, supports the Longleat MS 257 and Selden Supra 52 transmission, c. 1400–1410 northern composition, and Longleat witness context.

evidence.citation: Longleat Collections access page and Image and Video Request Form.
evidence.summary: States that some complete manuscript microfilm PDFs are purchasable for research, lists a current scanned-microfilm fee, limits new photography, prohibits copying/circulation/reproduction of research copies, and requires a separate publication/distribution licence.

evidence.citation: H. O. Coxe, Catalogus codicum mss. qui in collegiis aulisque Oxoniensibus hodie adservantur, vol. 1 (1852), Queen's College MS 213.
evidence.locator: Queen's MS 213; Vita begins f. 1 and following item begins f. 8
evidence.summary: Confirms a 50-leaf parchment codex copied by Nicholas Warde in 1449, bounds Vita prothoplasti Adae at ff. 1r–7v, and identifies the next item beginning f. 8.

evidence.citation: The Queen's College, Oxford, Special Collections.
evidence.summary: States that most medieval manuscripts have been microfilmed, research photography can be permitted, and reproduction permission must be requested; provides the current library contact.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Three Messengers of Death.
evidence.summary: Supports title, Vernon ff. 297v–298r locus, West Midlands textual context, DIMEV 5387, and collateral Simeon witness information.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library Imaging Services order, guidance, and terms pages.
evidence.summary: Supports fee-based image ordering, whole-object quotation requests, contact details, and the separation of image supply/private study from reproduction and publication licensing.

evidence.citation: Gonville and Caius College Library information and Copyright Policy.
evidence.summary: Provides the library enquiry route; the general policy describes supplied material as normally private-research only and publication as requiring written application/licence, subject to manuscript-specific confirmation.

open_questions.question: Why did the U-M/CME selection encode only the final pp. 207–224 volume-68 installment while omitting the earlier continuation beginning on p. 52?
next_steps: Check U-M project selection documentation and cataloging notes.
Keep the five encoded installments separate and do not call CME00063 the complete Nachträge serial.

open_questions.question: Can complete color facsimiles of CUL Gg.2.6, Dd.1.1, Ii.4.9, Ff.2.38, and Caius 174/95 be obtained under usable research and publication terms?
next_steps: Request the full current physical object from each institution, including all binding and manuscript surfaces.
Ask CUL to reconcile Dd.1.1 and Ff.2.38 foliation conflicts.
Ask Caius whether complete existing photography or microfilm is available and whether its general copyright policy applies.

open_questions.question: Will the British Library supply or restore complete digital objects for Cotton Caligula A II, Additional 22283 and 31042, Harley 4775, Egerton 876, Harley 2382, and Harley 4011?
next_steps: Submit one whole-object quotation request per current physical codex, not a cropped target request.
Ask whether Add MS 22283's cataloged complete digitization and the Harley legacy viewers can be restored.
Obtain written reuse terms separately from supply.

open_questions.question: Can complete facsimiles be obtained for Bodley 596, Douce 322, Douce 372, Rawlinson poetry 118, Rawlinson C. 499, and Rawlinson B. 408?
next_steps: Request each complete physical object through Bodleian Imaging Services or mediated copying.
Retain the complete provider selections for Bodley 596, Douce 322, and Douce 372 as partial only; do not mistake them for full manuscripts.
Map each target separately after whole-object delivery.

open_questions.question: Are complete existing surrogates available for Queen's College MS 213 and Longleat MS 257, and what physical surfaces do they cover?
next_steps: Ask Queen's for its complete existing microfilm/digital surrogate and written reproduction terms.
Ask Longleat whether MS 257 is eligible for the complete microfilm PDF and whether it includes all leaves, flyleaves, blanks, covers, and binding matter.
Do not classify either source as not digitized until the repositories answer.

open_questions.question: How should the remaining witness-locator conflicts be reconciled against the current physical objects?
next_steps: Confirm Dd.1.1 ff. 295r–296v versus old pp. 537–540 and ff. 258r–259v.
Confirm Ff.2.38 modern ff. 254r–257v versus Horstmann fol. 93ff.

Confirm Harley 4775 f. 264r versus catalog f. 262v; Egerton 876 f. 316 versus f. 321r; Bodley 596 f. 11v versus XML f. 12; Rawlinson poetry 118 ff. 113v–118v versus catalog ff. 4v–6v; and Add 31042 inferred f. 168v endpoint.
Record institutional answers without replacing historical locators.

open_questions.question: Do Kölbing's two transcripts, Furnivall's Bodley 596 transcript, or the unidentified Longleat transcript survive in an archive?
next_steps: Search Horstmann, Kölbing, and Furnivall papers and correspondence.
Compare any surviving transcript with manuscript images and the printed articles before asserting correction direction.

open_questions.question: What is the exact terminal folio of De ligno sanctae crucis in Queen's College MS 213, and does that codex have any demonstrated genealogical priority for the two English Adam versions?
next_steps: Inspect a complete Queen's MS 213 surrogate and map the item beginning f. 8.
Compare modern Vita Adae scholarship before treating Horstmann's 'Latin original' statement as established textual descent.

open_questions.question: Which complete or selected facsimile images may be redistributed in the public Git repository or commercial/noncommercial print-on-demand outputs?
next_steps: Keep every facsimile cache Git-ignored and publish links, hashes, coverage, and locators only until reuse authority is clear.

Treat Bodleian CC BY-NC, BSB NoC-NC, Lambeth permission requirements, Huntington's license-null object, CUL/BL/Caius/Queen's/Longleat supply terms, and underlying public-domain texts as separate layers.
Obtain written permission for any use outside the exact open or noncommercial terms.

open_questions.question: Do Lambeth or Huntington assert any open license or additional redistribution permission for the complete Sion and HM 114 image sequences?
next_steps: Ask Lambeth to interpret the LUNA rights value 'LPL' for private cache, public repository, and print-on-demand use.
Ask Huntington whether its no-permission policy for public-domain material includes the exact HM 114 digital photographs and bulk image redistribution.

open_questions.question: Why do BSB records describe the journal volumes as Berlin: E. Schmidt when the scanned issue title pages say Braunschweig: George Westermann?
next_steps: Check BSB serial-level cataloging history and later publication changes.
Continue to use the physical issue title pages for the five immediate edition imprints unless evidence establishes a reissue.
